title: RepoMind — Give Your Repository a Brain
subtitle: Ask your codebase anything. The answer is the path.
author: RepoMind

slides.title: RepoMind
slides.subtitle: Give your repository a brain

title.text: RepoMind — Give Your Repository a Brain

slides.notes: Hook. Turns any git repo into a typed knowledge graph and answers by walking relationships. Powered by Cognee.

slides.title: Your Repo Knows. You Can't Ask It.

title.text: Your Repo Knows. You Can't Ask It.

bodyPrimary.text: RepoMind turns commits, PRs, issues, people and chat into one typed knowledge graph, then answers natural-language questions by multi-hop traversal — so the answer comes with the path that proves it.

slides.title: We Mapped the Entire Cognee Repo

title.text: We Mapped the Entire Cognee Repo

slides.notes: Impact-first proof: real Cognee OSS repo, 128,899 typed edges.

items: Python 3.11 · FastAPI · Uvicorn — API + webhook server
Pydantic · GitPython · PyGithub — typed models & sources
Cognee — hybrid graph + vector memory (local or cloud)
Vanilla JS + force-graph — GitHub-themed web UI
pytest · ruff · uv — 109 tests, all offline

slides.title: One Idempotent Pipeline, Three Layers

bodySecondary.text: Git, GitHub, Slack, Discord — one Source interface.

body-4.text: One path: upsert, resolve refs, park/retry, tombstone.

slides.notes: One idempotent pipeline behind every trigger.

slides.title: Correctness-First By Design

body-20.text: Numeric refs never guessed — parked till resolved.

slides.notes: Accuracy over speed; all tested offline.

slides.title: Pluggable Memory: Pick Your Backend

bodySecondary.text: Offline, deterministic, zero AI deps — ideal for CI.

body-4.text: Local AI: Ollama + embedded kuzu, fully offline.

body-20.text: Hosted AI — just a key + URL, nothing to self-host.

slides.notes: Same typed graph across all three.

slides.title: Ask -> Answer -> Traversal Graph

bodyPrimary.text: Ask a question, get a structured, cited answer, and watch the traversal light up as a live graph. Zoom in, go fullscreen, and explore the 18,890-node Cognee brain.

slides.notes: DEMO CUE: ask 'who reviewed pull requests about the graph store'; show report, sources, traversal, fullscreen graph.

slides.title: What We Learned

items: Graph traversal beats vector RAG for explainable, cited answers
Idempotency + tombstones make ingestion safe to re-run anytime
Real repos are big — bounded ingestion and rate limits matter
One pluggable memory interface = swap local <-> cloud in a line

slides.notes: Correctness-first, then scaling to a 6,000-commit repo.
